Home › The GHZ State

The GHZ State

The GHZ State (Greenberger-Horne-Zeilinger) is a specific type of Multipartite Entanglement involving three or more qubits. It is often referred to as a "Schrödinger's Cat state" for multiple particles because it represents a macroscopic superposition of two distinct states across the entire system.

1. Definition and Maximality

A 3-qubit GHZ state is defined as: \[ |GHZ angle = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}(|000 angle + |111 angle) \] It is a Maximally Entangled state in the sense that it maximizes certain multipartite entanglement measures. However, it is also the most "fragile" form of entanglement; measuring a single qubit in the computational basis collapses the entire state into a purely classical configuration (either 000 or 111), destroying all quantum correlations between the remaining parties.

2. Non-Locality without Inequalities

The GHZ state is historically significant because it provides a "non-statistical" proof the failure of Local Realism. While Bell's Theorem relies on statistical averages over many trials, the GHZ paradox shows that for a single measurement, local hidden variable theories predict an outcome (e.g., +1) that is the exact opposite of the quantum prediction (-1). This "all-or-nothing" contradiction makes the GHZ state the ultimate tool for demonstrating the non-local nature of reality.

3. Eigenstates of Stabilizers

In the Stabilizer Formalism, the GHZ state is uniquely defined as the simultaneous eigenstate of the operators \( X_1 X_2 X_3 \), \( Z_1 Z_2 \), and \( Z_2 Z_3 \). This representation is crucial for Quantum Error Correction, where the GHZ state serves as a resource for detecting and correcting bit-flip and phase-flip errors across a distributed network.

Key Theoretical Identities

Interact with identities to explore their semantic depth.

  • Global Superposition [ Click to Expand Depth ]
    \[ |GHZ angle = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}(|0 angle^{\otimes n} + |1 angle^{\otimes n}) \]

    1. Physical Interpretation

    Global Superposition: A state where all particles are simultaneously in one of two distinct, mutually exclusive configurations.

    2. Symmetry & Origin

    Great Expansion: Symmetry derivation pending.

    3. Limits & Boundary

    Great Expansion: Limiting case analysis pending.

  • Non-Local Logic [ Click to Expand Depth ]
    \[ X_1 X_2 X_3 |GHZ angle = +1 |GHZ angle \]

    1. Physical Interpretation

    Non-Local Logic: The logical demonstration that GHZ correlations cannot be explained by any local hidden variable theory.

    2. Symmetry & Origin

    Great Expansion: Symmetry derivation pending.

    3. Limits & Boundary

    Great Expansion: Limiting case analysis pending.

  • Algebraic Definition [ Click to Expand Depth ]
    \[ Z_1 Z_2 |GHZ angle = +1 |GHZ angle \]

    1. Physical Interpretation

    Algebraic Definition: The set of operators that leave the GHZ state invariant, used to identify it in error-correction protocols.

    2. Symmetry & Origin

    Great Expansion: Symmetry derivation pending.

    3. Limits & Boundary

    Great Expansion: Limiting case analysis pending.
